name: barracks
summary: Rows of bunks or bedrolls for a standing force — personal gear at each rack, a weapons peg by the door, low steady lamplight.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangular — bunk rows want parallel walls

primary_palette.note: regulation barracks — stacked bunks in a row
single cots, no stacking, more personal space
bedrolls on the floor — warband, cultists, or poverty
officer barracks — better beds, fewer occupants
hammocks slung between pillars — mariners or slavers

story_prompts: mid-shift barracks — half the bunks occupied by sleeping guards
empty, beds made tight, everyone at their post
ransacked barracks after a mutiny, trunks thrown open
shared barracks of a motley mercenary band, gear of five nations
cultist dormitory, identical black robes folded on each bunk
anti_patterns: don't center a feature — barracks are perimeter rooms, the floor stays walkable
don't mix bed types randomly — a barracks has one bunk style
avoid heavy art or carpet; this is functional billet space
